1497	    impl<'a> TestChangeDetector<'a> {
1498	        fn new(pairs: &[(&'a str, Option<&'a str>, &[&'a str])]) -> Self {
1499	            let mut map = HashMap::new();
1500	            for (from, to, changed) in pairs {
1501	                map.insert(
1502	                    (*from, *to),
1503	                    changed
1504	                        .iter()
1505	                        .map(|s| {
1506	                            (
1507	                                PackageName::from(*s),
1508	                                // This is just a random reason,
1509	                                PackageInclusionReason::IncludedByFilter { filters: vec![] },
1510	                            )
1511	                        })
1512	                        .collect(),
1513	                );
1514	            }
1515	
1516	            Self(map)
1517	        }
1518	    }

content.text:
Implemented the fix.

What changed:
- Updated `crates/turborepo-scope/src/target_selector.rs` so source-control filter parsing now recognizes two-dot git ranges like:
  - `[origin/main..HEAD]`
  - `[main..feature]`
- These now parse as:
  - `from_ref = origin/main`
  - `to_ref = HEAD`
  - `merge_base = false`
  - `include_uncommitted = false`

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test for `[origin/main..HEAD]`.
- Confirmed it failed before the fix.
- Implemented the parser fix.
- Confirmed the test passed afterward.
- Removed the temporary regression test as requested.
- Re-ran the existing parser test suite successfully:

```text
cargo test -p turborepo-scope target_selector::test::parse_target_selector -- --nocapture
```

Result:

```text
30 passed; 0 failed
```

Final remaining source change is only the production fix in `target_selector.rs`. No commits were made.
